macro_segments.title: Introduction and Context of Partial Nezirus Declarations

micro_segments.title: Review of Previous Debate on Partial Nezirus (Beis Hillel vs. Beis Shammai)
Mishnah: Person Declares Nezirus from Wine While Being Offered a Cup
Halacha: Acceptance of Partial Nezirus Results in Full Nezirus

macro_segments.title: Story of the Drunk Woman and the Context-Dependent Interpretation of Intent

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Drunk Woman's Declaration of Nezirus from Wine
Sages' Ruling: Her Intent Was Limited to Forbidding This Specific Cup as a Neder
Problem Posed by Gemara: How Does This Story Contradict the Previous Ruling?
Gemara's Supplementation: Missing Clarifying Clause Between the Two Cases

macro_segments.title: Explanation of Drunk Woman's Case Through Pragmatic Intent

micro_segments.title: Gemara's Reasoning: Drunk Person Uses Strong Language to Discourage Further Pressure
Tosafos Elaboration: Drunk Person Faces Pressure to Drink; Others Pressure Him
Contrast with Sober Person: No Pressure to Drink, So Full Nezirus Language Indicates Full Intent

macro_segments.title: Nezirus Conditional on Non-Performance of Obligations

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Person Declares Nezirus with Explicit Conditions (Still Drinking Wine, Becoming Tamei)
Ruling: Conditions Are Invalid; Nezirus is Binding in Full

macro_segments.title: Nezirus Declarations With Mistaken Understanding of Obligations

micro_segments.title: Second Case: Person Unaware That Nezir Is Forbidden Wine
Debate: Chachamim Say Binding; Rabbi Shimon Says Not Binding
Third Case: Person Thought Chachamim Would Grant Dispensation (e.g., for Gravedigger)
Reversal: Chachamim Say Permissible; Rabbi Shimon Says Binding

macro_segments.title: Explanation of Position Reversal Through Partial vs. Full Acceptance

micro_segments.title: Second Case as Partial Nezirus: Person Only Accepted Non-Wine Nezirus

macro_segments.title: Nezirus Conditional on Non-Performance of Obligations (continued)

micro_segments.title: Razina's Explanation: Conditions Against Torah Are Nullified

macro_segments.title: Explanation of Position Reversal Through Partial vs. Full Acceptance (continued)

micro_segments.title: Earlier Debate: Chachamim Say Partial Becomes Full; Rabbi Shimon Says Partial Is Nothing
Third Case as Full Nezirus with Request for Hitur: Mirror Image of Acceptance
Explanation: Process of Kabbalah Parallels Process of Hatara
Gemara Suggests Nidrei Onesim (Coerced Vows) Framework for Third Case
Debate: Whether Nidrei Onesim Require Formal Hatarat Nedarim Process
Tosafos Alternative: Issue Is Ein Piv V'libo Shavin (Words ≠ Intent)

macro_segments.title: Nezirus with Accepting Responsibility to Bring Korbanot for Another Nazir

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Person Becomes Nazir and Accepts Responsibility to Bring Another's Korbanot
Smart Option: Two Nazirim Help Each Other, Each Bringing the Other's Korbanot
Gemara Question: If Second Person Only Says 'And Me,' Does It Cover Both Obligations?

macro_segments.title: Analysis of Partial Statements in Nazirus Declarations

micro_segments.title: Gemara Tests Whether 'And Me' Covers Full Statement or Only Part
Proof from Mishnah Text: Both Clauses Present Indicates Neither Is Superfluous
Rav Huna's Challenge: Alternative Reading Where V'ani Covers Everything
Rava's Refutation: Two Superceding Cases Suggest V'ani Alone Insufficient
Final Position: V'ani Must Be Supplemented to Create Full Halachic Effect

topical_tags.term: Matneh al mah shekatuv baTorah (conditions against Torah)
